description_long: This object consists of a group of six vases, each with a distinct classical form and smooth, unadorned surfaces. The vases vary in shape, including rounded, bulbous, and slender silhouettes, with some featuring narrow necks and others wide mouths. All are rendered in a uniform, pale beige or terracotta-like color, giving them a cohesive and minimalist appearance. The surfaces appear matte and finely finished, with no visible ornamentation or patterns. The vases are hollow, as indicated by their open tops, and are likely made from ceramic or clay. Their size and design suggest they are intended for decorative use in urban environments such as plazas, parks, or building entrances.
description: A set of six smooth, pale beige vases with varied classical shapes and open tops, designed for decorative urban placement.
description_view_0: Front view showing all six vases side by side, highlighting their distinct shapes and open tops.
description_view_1: Side view with the vases aligned in a row, showing their profiles and the variation in neck and body shapes.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the vases, mirroring the front but with the left-right order reversed.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the vases in profile with their open tops visible from a different angle.
